creative_brief: Hero image: founder's hands holding a box of mixed regenerative produce against a raw timber hub counter. Warm, natural light. No polish — real textures, real setting. Below the fold, portrait-style farm photography of named suppliers. Earthy palette, editorial feel, not startup glossy.
headline: I built a grocery store because I couldn't find one I trusted
subhead: I spent years reading labels that said 'farm fresh' while farmers went broke and my family ate food that travelled 2,400km to reach us. So I stopped waiting for supermarkets to change — and started building the alternative.
hero_copy: I'm not a food industry insider. I'm a parent who stood in Rachel Ward's farmhouse kitchen in Kempsey, NSW, and realised everything I thought I knew about 'fresh' was wrong. The vegetables came from soil I could touch. The farmer could tell me exactly what went into growing them. I drove home, opened my fridge, and couldn't trace a single item back to a single person. That's when I knew: the grocery store I wanted didn't exist yet. So I decided to build it.

sections.heading: The problem isn't bad food. It's a broken system.
sections.body:
Supermarkets weren't designed to connect you with farmers. They were designed to extract margin at every step. Zero transparency. Zero relationship. Zero accountability.

Farmers like the team at Bundarra Farm, who raise their pork with care. Collins Wild Caught, pulling wild-caught Alaskan salmon off the South Coast of NSW. Paris Creek Farms in Meadows, SA, producing carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy. Farmer Brown in NSW, doing eggs the right way. Little Yarran Farm, quietly growing food worth knowing about.

These people exist. They're extraordinary. But the current system buries them behind wholesalers, warehouses, and plastic wrap.

FarmThru changes the chain. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. Zero middlemen. Straight from farm to hub. You order online, collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday. That's it. No subscription box. No lock-in. An actual grocery store built on regenerative farms — priced for everyday families.

sections.heading: I didn't want customers. I wanted partners.
sections.body:
When I imagined FarmThru, I never pictured doing this alone. I pictured a community that owns a piece of the grocery store that pays farmers first.

That's why we're opening FarmThru to everyday investors through an equity crowdfunding campaign. Not venture capital. Not private equity. People like you — who believe values and money can finally point in the same direction.

You can join the waitlist now with a fully refundable $5 deposit. No commitment beyond that. You'll be first to know when the campaign goes live, and you'll have the chance to be part of something that doesn't exist yet in Australian grocery.

Be a customer. Be a partner. Or both. The waitlist is for anyone who wants in.

This is a genuine investment opportunity — which means it carries genuine risk. Your capital is at risk. Returns are not guaranteed. You should read the offer document carefully before making any decision. This is not financial advice.

sections.heading: We'll show you where your food comes from
sections.body:
That's not a tagline. It's a promise I made to myself in that Kempsey kitchen. Every product on FarmThru traces back to a real farm, a real person, a real practice. Because you deserve to know — and farmers deserve to be known.

Join the waitlist. Put your $5 refundable deposit down. And when the campaign opens, decide if this is the kind of grocery store you want to own a piece of.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru is planning an equity crowdfunding campaign. Any investment made through this campaign will be subject to risks, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. Returns are not guaranteed. Any information provided here is general in nature and does not constitute financial advice. You should consider your own circumstances and read the offer document in full before making any investment decision.
